publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 68-69. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: My Dearest Mate. A sad disappointment this morning, when I had certainly reckoned on a letter from Exeter!--
The house is very vacant without you, though I have no business to complain when the dear kind Hen is here.--
There is an enclosure of this morning written on smart pink paper for your mother,
and I think the hand is Emily's, but there is not a line for you. I strongly suspect she is affronted.--
I am delighted with the progress of the hot-house. miss D'Espourria & I stood in admiration of it today.--

The Hen has just brought down a letter for you, which she (naughty bird!) won't let me (poor little thing!) read.-- Do not believe any bad she may have said of me, for I assure you I am a very dear little thing!--
Rickman's Architecture, which came by the Cab. She cuts open all the new books she can find.-- I have sent my dressing case to Town to be done the best with which the case admits of.--

A leg has just come off one of the hideous screens in the sitting room, and the generous Hen insists on giving me some instead. I am only afraid you may have ordered some.-- I mean to give her a clock for Fordhood Chimney Piece for mine has left a sad
On Sunday we have no general post at Fordhook, but on Monday I expect to hear from you there. How I long to see your hand- writing! I am afraid I am but a silly little bird.

Lady George Murray offers to come here on Tuesday or Wednesday next. I have told her I shall then be away, but hope she can come later.-- The little Bird, with its young, chirps at ou, & says Good Bye.
Your most affecte A. A. K.
